data.title: Bitmine Immersion's 100K+ weekly ETH buy tops Dec pace, yet price stuck
data.description:
Ethereum (ETH-USD) is holding near $2.3K, up ~7% monthly, but the bigger story is Bitmine Immersion's (BMNR) massive accumulation strategy. 

As of April 19, 2026, the company holds ~4.98M Ethereum (ETH-USD) at ~$2,301 each, including 199 Bitcoin (BTC-USD), $200M in Beast Industries, $107M in Eightco Holdings (ORBS), and $1.12B in cash.Reportedly, this Ethereum (ETH-USD) staking now accounts for ~4.12% of the total supply (120.7M ETH), making Bitmine Immersion (BMNR) one of the largest ETH holders. 

Interestingly, 3.33M ETH (~$7.7B) is already staked, contributing ~67% of total holdings and generating steady yield momentum, as per PR Newswire.

The buying hasn’t slowed either. In the past week alone, the company added ~101.6K ETH, making it one of the fastest accumulations since December 2025—betting that the “mini crypto winter” is nearing its end.

Macro trends are supportive too. Ethereum (ETH-USD) is up ~41% from early February lows and has outperformed the S&P 500 by 2,280 bps during recent geopolitical tensions, strengthening its narrative as a “war-time store of value,” according to Tom Lee.

But here’s the twist—despite all this accumulation, Bitmine Immersion (BMNR) stock is down ~4% to ~$22.08, so the stock reaction raises a clear question—what is the market missing?

data.title: L3Harris wins $65M rocket motor contract for tactical missiles
data.description:
L3Harris Technologies (LHX) on Monday said it received a contract worth more than $65 million to produce solid rocket motors for the Army Tactical Missile System, or ATACMS, a long-range guided missile used by the U.S. military and allied forces.

The company said it will manufacture, test and deliver M124 rocket motors, igniters, exit cones and related components under the award. Deliveries are scheduled from 2027 through 2028.

The contract adds to L3Harris’ missile propulsion backlog as demand for precision-guided munitions remains elevated amid global rearmament and replenishment of U.S. stockpiles. For investors, the award also signals continued funding visibility for tactical missile programs and supports the company’s expansion in defense manufacturing capacity.

L3Harris has supplied propulsion systems for the Army’s ATACMS program for more than three decades, with production centered in Camden, Arkansas.

The Camden site produces more than 115,000 solid rocket motors annually across multiple programs, according to the company, and conducts more than 6,000 hot-fire tests each year. L3Harris said it is expanding the site with more than 20 new propulsion facilities to meet rising demand.

The company also manufactures ATACMS arm and firing devices at its Cincinnati, Ohio, facility.

data.title: RTX’s Raytheon delivers next-generation jammer pods to Royal Australian Air Force
data.description:
Raytheon, a business of RTX Corp. (RTX), on Monday said it delivered its first Next Generation Jammer pods to the Royal Australian Air Force under a joint development and production program with Australia and the U.S. Navy.

The airborne electronic warfare system is designed to disrupt enemy radar and communications systems. Raytheon said the initial delivery of shipsets took place ahead of schedule in September 2025, with additional deliveries planned through 2026.

The delivery highlights continued international demand for advanced electronic warfare systems as U.S. allies increase defense spending and seek interoperability with American platforms. For investors, the program reinforces RTX’s (RTX) position in a growing segment of defense procurement that can support backlog visibility and longer-term revenue from sustainment and upgrades.

The jammer pods are intended to improve survivability and mission effectiveness for military aircraft by helping crews operate with reduced detection risk.

Raytheon said it has worked with the U.S. Navy and Royal Australian Air Force since the program’s inception. In addition to deliveries, the company said it is providing deployment and maintenance support in Australia.

data.title: B2Gold cuts Q2 Goose mine production after fire, maintains full-year guidance
data.description:
B2Gold (BTG) down 1.4% in Monday's trading after reporting a fire that damaged part of the crushing circuit at its Goose mine in Nunavut, saying it anticipates Q2 gold production from the mine at 18K-20K oz, down from earlier expectations for ~29K oz.

However, B2Gold (BTG) said the damage from the April 16 fire was limited to the crushing circuit area, and it expects the impact of lower availability of crushed ore due to the fire will be limited to Q2, allowing it to maintain full-year gold production guidance for the Goose mine at 170K-230K oz.

B2Gold (BTG) said it has mobile crushers at the site, and its preliminary plan is to use them to feed crushed ore directly to the fine ore stockpile, while additional temporary crushing capacity will be taken to the site in the second quarter.

The miner estimated the repairs to the crushing circuit will be completed in Q3 and will coincide with its previously announced addition of a run-of-mine bin and apron feeder.

Earlier Monday, B2Gold (BTG) said it agreed to sell its 70% interest in the Fingold joint venture to Agnico Eagle Mines in a $325M deal.

data.title: Seized Iranian cargo ship highlights China trade links, sanctions risks
data.description:
The Iranian cargo vessel seized by U.S. forces in the Gulf of Oman over the weekend is part of a shipping network tied to trade with China, a key economic partner for Tehran, The Wall Street Journal reported Monday, citing shipping analysts.

The incident underscores growing enforcement risk around Iranian shipping routes and could tighten pressure on trade flows involving China, energy markets and regional logistics. For investors, renewed scrutiny of sanctions evasion networks may raise volatility in oil prices, shipping insurance and companies exposed to Middle East supply chains.

The ship, the MV Touska, had visited the southern Chinese port of Zhuhai twice in the six weeks before U.S. forces intercepted it Sunday while it was heading toward Iran’s Bandar Abbas port.

According to the Pentagon, the vessel ignored repeated warnings from the USS Spruance for about six hours. U.S. forces then fired at the engine-room area, disabled the ship and sent Marines aboard.

U.S. officials have not disclosed what cargo was on board. Analysts said the ship’s apparent attempt to break a U.S. blockade suggests it may have been carrying material considered strategically important.

The Touska is among a fleet of Iranian-linked vessels that continue operating despite U.S. and European sanctions, transporting oil, containerized goods and industrial cargo. It was sanctioned by Washington in 2018 during the Trump administration’s earlier pressure campaign against Iran.

Shipping records indicate the vessel is linked to an IRISL subsidiary. Other ships tied to the same operator have previously been associated with transporting Chinese-origin materials allegedly used in Iran’s missile program.

China said it was concerned by the seizure and called for restraint in the Strait of Hormuz, while reiterating opposition to unilateral sanctions.

The U.S. Navy said last week that sanctioned Iranian vessels, and ships suspected of carrying contraband such as weapons or military electronics, could be boarded, searched and seized.

data.title: RTX, GE Aerospace Q1 earnings on deck: What to expect
data.description:
When RTX (RTX) posts first quarter earnings on Tuesday, investors would focus on the aerospace and defense giant’s guidance amid strong demand for its missiles and defence equipment backed by rising military spending.

Wall Street expects Arlington, Virginia-based RTX to post EPS of $1.52, implying a rise of 3.4%, while revenue is expected to rise nearly 6% to $21.47B during the quarter.

Investors are turning their attention towards the aerospace and defence sector as tensions between U.S.-Israel and Iran continue to escalate, leading to a rise in demand for programs tied to missiles, radar systems, aircraft, and interceptors.

Along with being a prime contractor to the Pentagon’s Golden Dome missile defense initiative, RTX has recently signed contracts to supply its Patriot interceptors to Ukraine and the Netherlands. The stock has gained nearly 8% so far this year, outperforming the broader index.

“We view RTX as very well-positioned to see backlog growth in the quarter, which could provide increased investor confidence in 2026-2027 revenues as well,” said RBC analyst Ken Herbert, adding that he believes the company will raise its annual guidance.

Herbert further noted that “despite the current Administration’s focus on reducing dividends and stock repurchases for the primes, we believe the strong cash generation, and well-diversified nature of RTX’s business are reasons to own the stock.”

GE Aerospace (GE) is also set to report quarterly results on Tuesday, with Wall Street expecting the firm to post EPS of $1.60, representing a rise of over 7%. Meanwhile, revenue is expected to rise 19% to $10.71B during the quarter.

Baird expects “mixed” results for defense contractors in early 2026, while aerospace names continue to benefit from favorable sentiment, strong order books and production tailwinds.

Over the last two years, RTX has beaten both revenue and EPS estimates 100% of the time, while GE has beaten EPS estimates 100% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 63% of the time.

Over the last 3 months, EPS estimates for RTX have seen seven upward revisions, compared to three downward revisions. Revenue estimates have been revised upwards nine times versus three downward moves.

GE has seen five upward revisions for EPS estimates over the last three months, compared to three downward moves, while revenue estimates have seen six upward revisions versus no downward moves.

data.title: United Natural Foods is the most overbought consumer staples stock as Q1 earnings roll on
data.description:
As the market moves into the second week of Q1 earnings, we look at the most overbought stocks in the consumer staples sector.

Below is a list of the top 10 consumer staples stocks ranked by their RSI. The list includes companies from diverse industries within the sector, ranging from food distributors to personal care products.

The list is topped by United Natural Foods (UNFI), with an RSI of 69. Smithfield Foods (SFD), Interparfums (IPAR), and Target Corporation (TGT) follow closely behind, each with an RSI of 66. Casey’s General Stores (CASY) rounds out the top five with an RSI of 65.

Other notable entries include Performance Food Group Company (PFGC), PriceSmart (PSMT), and Central Garden & Pet Company (CENT). The RSI values for this top 10 list range from 69 down to 62, with Brown-Forman Corporation (BF.B) and Coty Inc. (COTY) each posting an RSI of 62.

The Relative Strength Index is a momentum oscillator that measures the velocity and magnitude of price changes, plotting them on a scale from 0 to 100. RSI compares the magnitude of recent gains to recent losses over a chosen lookback period, typically 14 days.

Here is the list:

  1. United Natural Foods, Inc. (UNFI), RSI: 69

  2. Smithfield Foods, Inc. (SFD), RSI: 66

  3. Interparfums, Inc. (IPAR), RSI: 66

  4. Target Corporation (TGT), RSI: 66

  5. Casey’s General Stores, Inc. (CASY), RSI: 65

  6. Performance Food Group Company (PFGC), RSI: 64

  7. PriceSmart, Inc. (PSMT), RSI: 63

  8. Central Garden & Pet Company (CENT), RSI: 63

  9. Brown-Forman Corporation (BF.B), RSI: 62

  10. Coty Inc. (COTY), RSI: 62

Consumer Staples ETFs: (XLP), (VDC), (IYK), (FSTA), (KXI), and (RSPS)

data.title: JD Vance, U.S. delegation head to Pakistan for another round of talks - reports
data.description:
Vice President JD Vance, along with the U.S. delegation, is reportedly expected to land in Islamabad, Pakistan, within hours.  

President Donald Trump said he was willing to meet senior Iranian leaders if the talks materialize properly, the New York Post reported. 

“We’re supposed to have the talks… So I would assume at this point nobody’s playing games,” Trump said in a brief interview with the Post. 

However, CNN reported that Vance has not yet left for Pakistan.

The U.S. president said a deal with Iran will be signed tonight, Trump had reportedly told Maria Bartiromo from Fox Business on Sunday.

He had also said that the U.S. had closed the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday as part of the ongoing naval blockade. 

Earlier, Iran had said it would not participate in a second round of talks with the United States, according to Iranian state media, which said Tehran objected to what it described as excessive and unrealistic demands from Washington.

Despite Tehran’s announcement, Trump had said U.S. negotiators were prepared to continue talks and planned to travel to Pakistan for another round of meetings.

The diplomatic setback comes as investors monitor disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical route for global oil shipments. Any prolonged impasse between Washington and Tehran could intensify concerns over energy supplies and regional stability.

“Well, I don’t want to get into that with you,” he told the Post when asked if the U.S. would escalate matters if a deal is not reached. “You can imagine. It wouldn’t be pretty,” he had added.

Dear Readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Data center demand drives upside for range of semis ahead of earnings: Susquehanna
data.description:
Data center infrastructure demand should drive a modest upside for a range of semiconductors for their first-quarter results and second-quarter outlooks, according to Susquehanna.

This includes AMD (AMD) and Intel (INTC) as AI workloads are boosting CPU demand. Arm's (ARM) hyperscaler CPUs are also expected to continue gaining share in cloud computing. ASIC suppliers being drawn into hyperscaler roadmaps has also proved positive for Broadcom (AVGO) and Marvell (MRVL). And of course, Nvidia (NVDA) rages full steam ahead with its Blackwell and Rubin revenue expected to surpass $1T by the end of 2027.

"For Optical/AI Networking, we (unsurprisingly) expect continued upside, driven by an ever-expanding 800G cycle and an emerging 1.6T cycle (and OCS/CPO)," said Susquehanna analysts, led by Christopher Roland, in an extensive investor report on Monday. "Nonetheless, the expanding networking TAM is also benefiting copper, and we expect the environment for AECs, LEs, and DSPs to remain constructive for all."

Optical companies such as Coherent (COHR), Lumentum (LITE), and Fabrinet (FN) all stand out as beneficiaries. Susquehanna increased its price target on these stocks to $425 from $250, to $1,100 from $550, and to $850 from $570, respectively. Susquehanna also nudged up Credo's (CRDO) price target to $200 from $170.

Outside the data center market, upside is more limited. Besides Apple (AAPL), Susquehanna expects the smartphone market to drag in 2026. It expects a year-over-year decline of 10% in smartphone shipments. Auto also appears weaker as Tesla's (TSLA) first-quarter deliveries were less than market expectations. 

For industrial names, the data center end market has provided a spark, prompting Susquehanna to edge up its price target on Texas Instruments (TXN) to $275 from $260, Microchip Technology (NCHP) to $95 from $90, and Analog Devices (ADI) to $475 from $400.

data.title: Quant check: Power stocks in focus as EVs, data center demand drive global electricity surge
data.description:
Global electricity consumption rose 3% last year, fueled in part by surging demand from electric vehicles and data centers, according to the International Energy Agency.

Electricity demand in 2025 grew around 2.3 times faster than total energy demand, the IEA said in its Global Energy Review. Demand from EVs and data centers grew at 38% and 17%, respectively, but industry, household appliances, and commercial buildings remained the main growth drivers.

In advanced economies, electricity demand expanded by a robust 1.6% year-over-year, with particularly strong growth in the United States. Data centers accounted for around 50% of total electricity demand growth in the U.S., with additional growth coming from the residential, industry, and transport sectors.

This aligns with projections in the IEA’s report Energy and AI report, which found that data centers are set to account for half of electricity demand growth in the United States to 2030.

Here’s a Quant check on leading power producers and their year-to-date performance:

  • PG&E Corporation (PCG) — Strong Buy (4.64), YTD 7.41%
